bio: Farzad Kamangar (1975–2010) was a Kurdish schoolteacher from Kamyaran who became an international symbol of political repression in Iran. Arrested in 2006, he was charged with moharebeh (enmity against God) for alleged membership in [PJAK](/orgs/pjak), charges he denied. His trial lasted only minutes and was condemned by Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch as lacking due process. He was secretly executed on 9 May 2010 in Evin prison, along with four other political prisoners, without his family or lawyer being notified. His letters from prison gained widespread circulation and he became a symbol of the Kurdish rights struggle in Iran.
editorial_notes: Executed 9 May 2010. Trial widely condemned as unfair. Symbol of Kurdish political repression.
